title: Monetary policy: foundational concepts
description: Are you ready to be the next chair of the Federal Reserve? Practice your skills applying monetary policy here!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would be the FIRST change to occur when a central bank conducts open market purchases of bonds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

First Bank is one of many banks in the banking system in Hamsterville. The central bank of Hamsterville sells bonds to First Bank.

**What is the immediate impact of the central bank's action?**
[[☃ radio 1]]

**When the central bank of Guilder sells bonds, which of the following will occur?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Reserves decrease; the monetary base decreases
Reserves decrease; the monetary base increases
Reserves do not change; the monetary base increases
Reserves increase; the monetary base decreases
Reserves decrease; the monetary base does not change

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens when a central bank buys bonds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is a monetary policy tool that would be used by a central bank to close a recessionary gap?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

As a result of an output gap, the unemployment rate in Hamsterville is lower than the natural rate of unemployment.

**Which of the following is an open market operation that could be used to close the gap?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is a contractionary monetary policy tool that is NOT an open market operation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is an expansionary monetary policy tool that is NOT an open market operation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The reserve requirement in Petmeckistan is $20\%$.

**If the central bank buys $\$1000$ in bonds, what is the maximum possible change in the money supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The central bank in Hamsterville has bought $\$100$ in bonds from a bank. The reserve requirement is $25\%$.

**What is the maximum possible change in the money supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The reserve requirement in Florin is $20\%$. The Central Bank of Florin buys $\$2{,}000$ worth of bonds using open market operations.

**Which of the following describes the impact of the open market purchase of bonds on the monetary base and the money supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The final increase in the money supply will depend on the money multiplier. The money multiplier is:

$MM=\dfrac{1}{RR}$

Where
$RR=\text{reserve requirement}$
If the reserve requirement is $20\%$, the money multiplier is:

$\begin{align}MM&=\dfrac{1}{RR}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{20\%}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{0.2}\\\\
&=5\end{align}$
The money multiplier represents the maximum change in the money supply from a change in the monetary base. If the money multiplier is $5$, every $\$1$ change in the monetary base will lead to at most a $\$5$ change in the money supply.
The maximum change in the money supply will be:

$5 \times \Delta MB$
The maximum change in the money supply will be: 

$5 \times \$2{,}000 = \$10{,}000$

choices.content: The monetary base increases by exactly $\$2{,}000$; the money supply increases by up to $\$10{,}000$
The monetary base increases by at least $\$2{,}000$; the money supply increases by at least $\$10{,}000$
The monetary base increases by less than $\$2{,}000$; the money supply increases by at least $\$10{,}000$
The monetary base increases by exactly $\$2{,}000$; the money supply increases by exactly $\$10{,}000$
The monetary base increases by less than $\$2{,}000$; the money supply increases by up to $\$10{,}000$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the relationship between the change in the monetary base and the change in the money supply that occurs when a central bank buys bonds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The change in the money supply is greater than the change in the monetary base
The change in the money supply is equal to the change in the monetary base
The change in the money supply is unrelated to the change in the monetary base
The change in the money supply is always the change in the monetary base times the money multiplier
The change in the money supply is always less than the change in the monetary base

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following interest rates is a central bank MOST LIKELY to target directly when using monetary policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best describes the federal funds rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The interest rate banks charge other banks for short-term loans
The interest rate banks charge other banks for long-term loans
The interest rate that the Federal Reserve charges banks for short-term loans
The fraction of deposits that a bank must keep deposited at the Federal Reserve
The interest rate that the Federal Reserve pays for required reserves

assessment_items.question_content:
**When the Central Bank of Montoya increases the money supply, what is the impact?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Financial capital will flow into Montoya

assessment_items.question_content:
The Federal Reserve is the central bank of the United States. 

**Which of the following is the target of an open market operation by the Federal Reserve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is an example of expansionary monetary policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The central bank of Hamsterville decided to decrease the money supply.

**Which of the following is the most likely reason that the central bank decreased the money supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It was concerned about high inflation.
It was concerned about high unemployment.
It was concerned that aggregate demand is too low.
It wanted interest rates to decrease.
It wanted to engage in expansionary fiscal policy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the rate that banks in the United States charge each other for overnight loans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The Bank of Dodge had several large withdrawals and its reserves have fallen below its required reserves. Other banks are unwilling to lend it money, so it has turned to the emergency lending division of the Federal Reserve for an overnight loan.

**What is the name of the interest rate this bank will be charged for an overnight loan from the Federal Reserve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The IOER (interest rate on excess reserves)
